items.title: Opus 4.8 in 41 days; Mythos weeks away — release velocity as enterprise moat
items.idea: Anthropic shipped Opus 4.8 just 41 days after 4.7, with a new 'dynamic-workflow tool' targeting enterprise multi-hundred-agent orchestration. Mythos — the next, more powerful model — is still pending cybersecurity clearances with governments and major customers before release. The 41-day cadence between frontier flagships is now faster than most enterprise procurement cycles.
items.angle: The lab that ships fastest sets the standard before anyone can evaluate alternatives. Opus 4.8 is the SKU; the enterprise orchestration layer is the moat being built.
items.tweetA: 41 days between Anthropic flagship releases. The model release cadence is now faster than enterprise procurement cycles — the labs that ship fastest lock in standards before anyone can evaluate alternatives. Opus 4.8 is the product. 100-agent orchestration is the moat.
items.tweetB: Anthropic shipping Opus 4.8 + building Mythos + deploying a dynamic-workflow tool for 100-agent enterprise flows. This is not a model company anymore. It is becoming the enterprise orchestration layer. The model is just the SKU.

items.title: GitHub Copilot losing AI coding lead to Claude Code and Cursor — Microsoft fighting for relevance
items.idea: Microsoft is releasing a new coding model at next week's Build conference to defend Copilot, which has lost ground to Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, and Cursor (potentially being acquired by SpaceX/xAI). Copilot was the product that first put AI coding on the map. The comeback attempt is about whether Microsoft can re-anchor developers before agentic-coding standards set without it.
items.angle: The lab that invents a category doesn't always own it. Copilot went from monopoly to contested in 18 months. Build announcements are a Hail Mary, not a strategy.
items.tweetA: GitHub Copilot invented AI coding as a category. Now it is losing to Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex. The company that put AI coding on the map is fighting for relevance in its own backyard. $MSFT Build next week is a Hail Mary, not a strategy.
items.tweetB: Cursor potentially being acquired by SpaceX/xAI. Microsoft rebuilding Copilot. Google, Anthropic, OpenAI all have coding products. The AI coding market went from Copilot monopoly to fully contested in 18 months. That is the fastest category disruption I have tracked.

items.title: Altman vs Amodei AI narrative split is pre-IPO positioning, not philosophy
items.idea: Both OpenAI and Anthropic are preparing mega-IPOs later this year, after SpaceX/xAI goes public at $1.75T+ in June. The optimist (Altman) vs cautious (Amodei) framing is becoming a branding choice as much as a worldview — attracting different investor bases ahead of the roadshow. These public debates are actively worsening mainstream and regulator concerns about AI.
items.angle: The doomer-vs-accelerationist debate is a $100B roadshow in disguise. One narrative sells to growth funds, the other to ESG funds. Neither CEO is wrong; both are rational.
items.tweetA: Altman is bullish on AI. Amodei is cautious. Both are prepping IPOs. This is not a philosophical debate — it is two different investor marketing strategies. One sells to growth funds, one to ESG. SpaceX/xAI goes public at $1.75T+ next month. Then OpenAI. Then Anthropic.
items.tweetB: The AI safety vs AI acceleration debate is actually the largest pre-IPO roadshow in history. Three companies arguing publicly about the future while collectively raising $100B+. The audience is not researchers — it is institutional allocators.

items.title: Demis Hassabis turns more optimistic: AGI possible before 2030
items.idea: Google DeepMind head Demis Hassabis — historically the most science-first, cautious voice on AGI timelines — is now sounding more optimistic about AGI arriving before 2030. This is notable as a pull-forward from one of the few researchers who consistently resisted near-term hype. The timetable debate matters less for the date than for the capex, talent, and policy decisions being sized against it today.
items.angle: Track who changes their mind, not who was always bullish. Hassabis moving means the $500B in hyperscaler spend over 5 years is being re-justified in real time.
items.tweetA: Demis Hassabis — the most cautious, science-first voice in AGI — now says before 2030. I track who changes their mind, not who was always bullish. This is a data point worth more than 100 accelerationist tweets.
items.tweetB: The AGI timeline debate matters less for the date than for the capex committed today. If Hassabis now agrees 2030 is plausible, then $500B in hyperscaler spend over 5 years is not over-investment — it is appropriately discounted. Re-price the AI buildout.

items.title: Robinhood gives retail AI trading tools; hedge funds deploying AI investment systems
items.idea: Financial services is now seeing both retail (Robinhood) and institutional (hedge funds) adoption of AI for trading and investment. Finance is the natural early AI vertical — high data density, clear feedback loops, real money on outcomes. The open question is how regulators and retail investors absorb AI-driven trading at mainstream scale.
items.angle: Finance is always first. What happens here in 2026 will happen in healthcare, legal, and insurance in 2028-29. Mark the adoption curve.
items.tweetA: Robinhood just gave retail investors AI trading tools. The question nobody is asking: when AI executes $1T+ in retail trades, what happens to market microstructure? Who is modeling the correlation spike when every retail AI hits the same signal simultaneously?
items.tweetB: Finance is always the first AI vertical — fastest feedback loops, clearest ROI. AI in trading 2026 → AI in legal 2027 → AI in medicine 2028. We are at the Robinhood-gives-retail-AI stage. Mark the calendar. The adoption curve runs through money.

items.title: Pope Leo issues 42,000-word AI encyclical; Anthropic adds cautionary addendum
items.idea: Pope Leo has written a 42,000+ word papal document on AI risks — longer than most AI safety papers — which explicitly expands global debates over AI without the tech industry's optimism. Anthropic added a cautionary addendum to the encyclical. The Catholic Church has 1.3B members and significant influence over 130+ governments, particularly in Latin America, Africa, and Southern Europe.
items.angle: AI regulation outside the US and China will run through this document. It is a regulatory pre-condition for 2B+ people.
items.tweetA: Pope Leo just wrote 42,000 words on AI — longer than most AI safety papers. The Catholic Church has 1.3B members and influence over 130+ governments. AI regulation in Latin America, Africa, and Southern Europe will run through this document. Ignore it at your peril.
items.tweetB: Anthropic added a cautionary addendum to the Pope's AI encyclical. Think about what it means that a frontier AI lab is now co-authoring papal documents. The AI alignment debate just got a new venue — and this one has 1.3 billion subscribers.

items.title: Iran ceasefire in limbo: Trump 'couple of days to think'; Iran says no deal; Eurasia Group: 4-to-1 Iranian win
items.idea: Proposed MOU terms: unrestricted Hormuz shipping + all Iranian mines removed within 30 days + 60-day ceasefire extension + nuclear talks. But Iran's Revolutionary Guard struck a US air base Thursday, US struck back, and Iran says nothing is finalized. Eurasia Group rates the deal as a 4-to-1 victory for Iran vs the US. The 90-day war is the longest US Middle East conflict since the Iraq invasion.
items.angle: Weekend gap risk is real and multi-asset: Trump decision + thin liquidity + first 24/7 CME crypto session = oil, crypto, and equity vol event by Monday open.
items.tweetA: Iran deal: Trump says a couple of days to think. Iran's Revolutionary Guard hit a US air base Thursday. US struck back Thursday. Eurasia Group rates the deal 4-to-1 in Iran's favor. The Monday gap risk is not priced. $VIX $BTC $CL_F
items.tweetB: Hormuz closed 84+ days. Now Iran negotiates reopening via a deal Eurasia Group calls a 4-to-1 Iranian win — unrestricted shipping, mine removal, nuclear talks but no uranium disposal. If this closes, Brent $92 goes to $70s. If it breaks, $110. Weekend vol is the trade.

items.title: Brent $92 — worst month since 2020; Hormuz reopening could trigger energy collapse per NEC director
items.idea: Brent ~$92, WTI ~$88-90, worst month for oil since 2020 despite Hormuz handling ~20% of world oil supply pre-war being closed for 84+ days. If the deal holds and mines are removed within 30 days, Hassett (NEC Director) said the ceasefire could create 'negative inflation via energy collapse.' Energy producers OXY/EOG/FANG/CVX already extending monthly losses.
items.angle: The market has already repriced much of the war risk out. The peace dividend in oil may be larger than bulls expect if 20% of world supply comes back simultaneously.
items.tweetA: Brent $92. Worst month for oil since 2020. A deal that reopens Hormuz — which carried 20% of world oil pre-war — could take Brent to the $70s. The NEC director literally said this creates negative inflation via energy collapse. $OXY $CVX $EOG $FANG
items.tweetB: 90-day war, Hormuz closed, 20% of world oil supply offline — and oil is STILL down to its worst month since 2020. The market already priced in the war months ago. The ceasefire peace dividend in oil is largely spent at $92. The downside from here is the real trade.

items.title: April PCE 3.8% YoY (highest since May 2023); real personal income -1.1% — stagflation confirmed
items.idea: April PCE hit 3.8% YoY, the highest since May 2023. Core PCE 3.3%. Real personal income fell -1.1% YoY — the first sustained negative print. S&P is simultaneously approaching its 9th straight weekly gain near record highs. The data was ignored entirely by equities — 'good data doesn't matter' regime is in full force, and now bad data doesn't either.
items.angle: When both good AND bad data fail to move equities, that is peak euphoria. Warsh inherits this on June 16-17.
items.tweetA: PCE 3.8% — highest since 2023. Real personal income -1.1% YoY — first sustained negative print. S&P about to log its 9th straight weekly gain. This is not a soft landing. It is a market that has decided fundamentals are irrelevant until they are not.
items.tweetB: Stagflation checklist: inflation above target, real income negative, housing sales -10.8% YoY, 30Y above 5%. And S&P at record highs. Warsh steps into his first FOMC on June 16. He inherits a market that has priced perfection into an imperfect economy.

items.title: S&P 9th straight weekly gain; 20% rally in 10 weeks; stock-bond correlation at 20-year high
items.idea: S&P on pace for its 9th straight weekly advance — longest streak since 2023 — with a nearly 20% rally from March lows, a move matched only a few times in 40 years. But 30Y yield held above 5%, and the S&P-Treasury correlation is at a 20-year high, meaning portfolio diversification has collapsed. When stocks and bonds move together, there is no safe haven except cash and gold.
items.angle: The 60/40 portfolio is broken. Gold at $4,415 is the last hedge standing, and it is being sold on ceasefire optimism. That is the buy signal.
items.tweetA: S&P +20% in 10 weeks. Stocks and bonds moving TOGETHER at 20-year-high correlation. When your 60/40 portfolio has zero diversification left, the only hedge is duration or cash. And Warsh has not even held his first FOMC yet.
items.tweetB: 9 straight weekly gains. 30Y above 5%. PCE 3.8%. Real income negative. The bond market knows something equity investors are choosing not to hear. The two cannot both be right simultaneously.

items.title: Warsh Day 7 as Fed Chair — first FOMC June 16-17 is the real test
items.idea: Kevin Warsh is now 7 days into his tenure as Fed Chair. He inherits: PCE 3.8%, real income -1.1%, 30Y above 5%, S&P +20% in 10 weeks, and stocks/bonds correlated at a 20-year high. His first FOMC is June 16-17. Dovish reads as behind the curve; hawkish unwinds 9 weeks of equity gains. BoJ and ECB yield caps described as a 'permanent force for yields to rise' in market commentary.
items.angle: The trap is symmetric. Any clear signal creates the wrong reaction. The most likely outcome is artful ambiguity — which the market will initially love, then punish.
items.tweetA: Warsh Day 7. Inherits: PCE 3.8%, real income -1.1%, 30Y above 5%, S&P +20% in 10 weeks. First FOMC: June 16-17. Dovish = inflation wins. Hawkish = 9 weeks of equity gains unwind. There is no neutral option here. Welcome to the chair.
items.tweetB: The new Fed Chair's inaugural FOMC comes with the most confused macro backdrop in years. BoJ and ECB yield caps are permanently pushing global yields higher. Warsh cannot cut without losing credibility on day 17. Watch June 16-17 very carefully.

items.title: $7.5B Deribit expiry today — BTC and ETH both trading BELOW max pain; 9th straight ETF outflow day
items.idea: $7.5B in BTC+ETH options expire today on Deribit. BTC max pain $75K (currently $73,525), ETH max pain $2,200 (broke $2,000 intraday). BTC has 84,112 contracts ($6.2B), ETH has 643,639 contracts ($1.29B). BTC ETFs are in their 9th consecutive day of outflows — $228.88M on May 28, $2B+ since May 14. CoinGlass liquidity clusters at $71-72K represent the next downside magnet.
items.angle: Mechanical expiry pressure + 9 consecutive ETF outflow days + thin weekend liquidity + first 24/7 CME session = most complex crypto weekend setup in months.
items.tweetA: BTC $73,525. Max pain $75,000. ETH broke $2,000. Max pain $2,200. $7.5B options expire today with both assets below max pain. BTC ETFs: 9th straight outflow day, -$2B+ since May 14. Next liquidity cluster: $71-72K. The math is not bullish into the weekend.
items.tweetB: CME just launched 24/7 crypto futures. VanEck just launched spot BNB ETF. Infrastructure is being built for an asset that is correcting into its 9th consecutive ETF outflow day. Either this is dumb money building into weakness, or the structural upgrade IS the accumulation signal.

items.title: CME 24/7 crypto futures launch today + VanEck spot BNB ETF — weekend gap eliminated
items.idea: CME launched 24/7 crypto futures AND options trading today, eliminating the weekend gap that has been a structural feature of crypto markets for a decade. Simultaneously, VanEck launched the first US spot BNB ETF 'VBNB' on Nasdaq (0.39% fee, Anchorage custody). These are the two biggest structural changes to crypto market microstructure in years, happening the same day as a major options expiry and live ceasefire negotiations.
items.angle: Every volatility model that priced in weekend thin liquidity needs repricing. The crypto weekend gap premium is dead. Re-price every derivatives strategy built around it.
items.tweetA: Two things changed crypto market structure forever today: CME launched 24/7 futures (RIP weekend gap), and VanEck launched spot BNB ETF on Nasdaq. The infrastructure of crypto is becoming indistinguishable from TradFi. That is the entire long-term thesis in one sentence.
items.tweetB: No more Monday morning crypto gaps. CME is now 24/7. The weekend liquidity premium that powered a decade of gap-fade strategies is dead. Re-price every volatility model that assumed weekends equal thin liquidity. $BTC $ETH

items.title: DELL +28%, SNOW +25%, Cisco AI pipeline $5B → $9B — AI capex cycle re-accelerating
items.idea: DELL Q1 blowout on both lines, raised FY guidance on AI server demand — largest gain since 2018. Snowflake +25% on $6B AWS Graviton deal plus Q1 beat. Cisco raised its AI orders pipeline to $9B from $5B, an 80% quarterly increase. Three separate companies confirming the same thing in the same week: AI infrastructure spending is not slowing.
items.angle: The DeepSeek efficiency narrative that crashed AI infra stocks in January is being systematically invalidated. Every week of data like this closes the bear case.
items.tweetA: DELL +28%. SNOW +25%. Cisco AI pipeline raised $5B to $9B. Three companies confirming the same thing in one week: AI infrastructure spend is NOT slowing. Anyone who shorted the capex cycle on DeepSeek efficiency just got wrecked again. $DELL $SNOW $CSCO
items.tweetB: Cisco AI orders pipeline: was $5B. Now $9B. That is an 80% increase in the forward pipeline in one quarter. When Cisco — the backbone of enterprise networking — sees $9B coming, the capex cycle has years of runway left.

items.title: Samsung 45K-worker strike Day 4 of 18 — HBM chokepoint at NVDA $91B Q2 demand
items.idea: Samsung's 45,000-worker strike is in its 4th day of an 18-day action. Samsung is a critical supplier of HBM (high-bandwidth memory) to NVIDIA, which faces $91B in Q2 demand. HBM is the single biggest bottleneck in AI compute today. A prolonged strike could delay NVIDIA shipments and affect hyperscaler capex timelines at the exact peak demand moment.
items.angle: The AI buildout has a supply chain risk embedded in Korean labor negotiations that almost no one is monitoring. 14 more days of strike = real risk.
items.tweetA: Samsung 45,000-worker strike Day 4. NVDA has $91B in Q2 demand. Samsung makes HBM — the memory that makes AI chips work. If this goes the full 18 days, the AI buildout hits a supply wall that nobody is talking about. $NVDA $AMD $MU
items.tweetB: HBM is the single biggest bottleneck in AI compute. Samsung supplies a critical share of it. 45K workers on strike, Day 4 of 18. The AI rally is priced on demand. The supply chain risk is not. Watch this.

items.title: SpaceX $75B IPO June 11-12 — 3x largest US IPO ever; Tesla-SpaceX merger talks emerge
items.idea: SpaceX S-1 is being absorbed. Pricing target June 11-12. $75B raise = 3x the largest US IPO in US history. $1.5T+ valuation. Starship test scheduled Friday. CNBC reports Tesla + SpaceX merger talks — combined entity would hold $3.3B BTC as world's 5th-largest corporate BTC treasury.
items.angle: Every institutional PM is doing allocation math right now. $75B drawn from secondary markets in under 2 weeks is a massive liquidity event for equities.
items.tweetA: SpaceX pricing June 11-12. $75B raise equals 3x the largest US IPO in history. $1.5T+ valuation. Every institutional PM is doing allocation math right now. $75B gets pulled from secondary markets in under 2 weeks. Model what that does to equity positioning.
items.tweetB: SpaceX IPO June 11-12. Tesla-SpaceX merger talks. Starship test Friday. xAI merger would create the 5th-largest corporate BTC treasury at $3.3B. This is 3 separate market-moving stories happening simultaneously in one corporate structure.

items.title: MSTR converting convertible debt to equity over 3-6 years — removes BTC forced-sell risk
items.idea: Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) announced a plan to equitize its convertible debt over 3-6 years rather than repaying it at maturity. This removes the biggest structural overhang on BTC — the risk of forced selling to meet debt obligations. Strategy holds 818,334 BTC at an average cost of $75,700 (above current price of $73,525). Combined with Tesla-SpaceX merger potentially creating a $3.3B corporate BTC treasury, supply is being structurally tightened.
items.angle: Saylor is underwater AND defusing the debt bomb. Whether this reads as bullish conviction or balance-sheet desperation is the key question.
items.tweetA: MSTR converting all convertible debt to equity over 3-6 years. That removes the biggest overhang on BTC — forced selling from debt maturity. Tesla-SpaceX merger adds a $3.3B corporate BTC treasury. Supply is tightening structurally while price corrects. Tension.
items.tweetB: Strategy holds 818,334 BTC at $75,700 avg cost. BTC is at $73,525. Saylor is underwater AND converting all converts to equity simultaneously. Bullish conviction or balance-sheet necessity? The answer to that question is the BTC trade.

items.title: Gold $4,415 (-1.5%) selling on ceasefire optimism — while PCE prints 3.8%
items.idea: Gold fell -1.5% to $4,415, silver -2.2% to $73.27, both selling on ceasefire risk-on optimism. Yet April PCE simultaneously printed 3.8% YoY — the highest since 2023. The inflation hedge is being sold into the most inflationary print in years. Gold is trading the geopolitical risk premium, not the inflation premium.
items.angle: When the Iran deal stumbles (Eurasia Group rates it 4-to-1 Iranian win, likely to face domestic opposition), the geopolitical bid in gold returns on top of a 3.8% PCE floor.
items.tweetA: Gold selling off 1.5% on ceasefire optimism. PCE just printed 3.8% — highest since 2023. Real personal income -1.1%. The inflation hedge is being sold on peace hopes into the most inflationary data in 3 years. Buy the peace-deal dip in gold.
items.tweetB: Gold $4,415 down on Iran deal. Silver $73 down. PCE 3.8% ignored. When the geopolitical bid fades and inflation data reasserts, gold re-rates against a 3.8% PCE floor. The Eurasia Group said this deal is 4-to-1 Iranian. It may not hold.

items.title: SaaSpocalypse v2: Salesforce light + Zscaler -19% + Intuit 17% workforce cut
items.idea: Three major SaaS companies simultaneously in distress — Salesforce guidance light, Zscaler -19%, Intuit cutting 17% of its workforce. The pattern is structural: AI is replacing the workflows that multiple SaaS seats used to serve. The SaaS multiple compression of 2022 was a valuation story; the 2026 version is revenue.
items.angle: Intuit does tax prep, accounting, and financial software — exactly the workflows AI automates first. This is not a macro softness story. It is an AI displacement story.
items.tweetA: Salesforce light. Zscaler -19%. Intuit cutting 17% of workforce. SaaS was the safe harbor of tech investing for a decade. AI does not renew SaaS contracts — it replaces them. The 2022 compression was valuation. The 2026 version is revenue. $CRM $ZS $INTU
items.tweetB: Intuit is cutting 17% of staff. Intuit does tax prep, accounting, and financial software — exactly the workflows AI automates first. The SaaS platform era is being compressed at the product layer, not the macro layer. This does not recover when rates fall.

items.title: Mollick: enterprises burn $500M in AI tokens in a single month
items.idea: Ethan Mollick reports that companies are exhausting AI token budgets rapidly — $500M spent internally in a single month at some enterprises. This is the demand-side data point justifying hyperscaler AI capex: at $500M/month, that is $6B annualized from one enterprise deployment at scale.
items.angle: Every finance department is now getting an AI infrastructure line item they did not budget for. The ROI question is not abstract — it is being tested with $500M/month in real money.
items.tweetA: Mollick: enterprises burn $500M in AI tokens in a single month. That is $6B annualized from one deployment at scale. This is the number that explains why hyperscalers are spending $100B+ on AI capex. The demand is real and the budgets are being exhausted.
items.tweetB: Corporate AI token spend: $500M per month at enterprise scale. Every CFO is now getting an AI infrastructure bill they did not budget. This ends one of two ways: cost discipline kills adoption, or AI delivers ROI that dwarfs the spend. Which way it breaks is the investment question of 2026.

items.title: Trump drone industrial policy EO — UMAC/RCAT/AVEX/ONDS up 23-57% Thursday
items.idea: Trump signed a drone industrial policy executive order. Stocks UMAC, RCAT, AVEX, and ONDS rallied 23-57% Thursday. The 90-day Iran war has proven drone warfare as the decisive tactical technology. The policy creates a domestic industrial base with pricing power at the exact moment of war-tested demand proof.
items.angle: Defense + drone plays sit at the intersection of AI autonomy, domestic manufacturing, and war-tested demand. Three of the strongest policy tailwinds of 2026 converge here.
items.tweetA: Trump drone industrial policy EO: UMAC +23%, RCAT +57%, AVEX, ONDS all screaming Thursday. 90-day Iran war proved drones are the decisive weapon. Now the US is building a domestic industrial base for them. $UMAC $RCAT $AVEX $ONDS
items.tweetB: Drones just won a 90-day war. Trump signs an industrial policy to build them domestically. RCAT +57% in a day. This is AI autonomy + reshoring + defense tech in one trade. Three of the strongest 2026 policy tailwinds converging on the same ticker.

items.title: Senate CLARITY Act faces 100+ amendments; Warren leads 40+
items.idea: The CLARITY Act — crypto's major regulatory clarity legislation — is facing 100+ amendments in the Senate. Elizabeth Warren leads a coalition filing 40+ amendments alone. Markets have been pricing in regulatory clarity in 2026 as a crypto tailwind. A bill stalled by 100+ amendments means that timeline is at risk.
items.angle: CME is launching 24/7 infrastructure and VanEck is launching ETFs simultaneously. The market is moving without Washington — but the institutional adoption narrative still requires a regulatory foundation.
items.tweetA: The CLARITY Act got hit with 100+ Senate amendments — Warren alone filed 40+. Markets are pricing regulatory clarity in 2026. If this bill stalls, the institutional adoption narrative loses its foundation just as CME launches 24/7 crypto and VanEck launches BNB ETF.
items.tweetB: 100+ amendments on CLARITY. CME launched 24/7 crypto. VanEck launched spot BNB ETF. The infrastructure is being built while legislation is being blocked. The market is not waiting for Washington — but eventually the regulatory gap will matter.

items.title: China escalating digital yuan push as ceasefire-era dollar softens
items.idea: As the Iran ceasefire creates a risk-on, dollar-softening environment, China is accelerating its digital yuan (e-CNY) push. The strategic timing is notable: if Hormuz reopens under a US-brokered deal and China is first to settle post-war energy trade in e-CNY, it threads the needle of de-dollarization using an American peace dividend.
items.angle: De-dollarization via Hormuz reopening. The US brokers the peace; China monetizes the settlement.
items.tweetA: China is accelerating digital yuan deployment as the ceasefire softens the dollar. If Hormuz reopens and China is first to settle oil in e-CNY, they thread the de-dollarization needle using an American peace deal. The US just created an opening China is walking through.
items.tweetB: Iran deal reopens Hormuz. Dollar softens on risk-on. China immediately pushes digital yuan harder. Whoever denominates post-war energy trade wins the long game. The US is solving the military problem while China is solving the monetary problem.

items.title: US and China both unilaterally pull back in AI race simultaneously
items.idea: Both the US and China are taking unilateral steps backward in their AI race against each other. Parekh frames this as 'Govt AI Kryptonite' — government interference creating simultaneous friction in both superpowers' AI development programs. This creates a vacuum in the global AI race that middle-tier players (UAE, Saudi Arabia, UK, France, India) may fill.
items.angle: If both superpowers decelerate simultaneously, the geopolitical AI race becomes multipolar faster than anyone modeled. Sovereign AI funds in the Gulf start to look more interesting.
items.tweetA: Both the US and China just took unilateral steps backward in their AI race. Simultaneously. Watch what fills the vacuum: UAE, Saudi Arabia, India, UK labs. The AI geopolitical race may become multipolar faster than anyone expected.
items.tweetB: The most consequential AI safety intervention of 2026 may have come not from safety researchers but from politicians in two capitals who do not understand the technology. The labs benefit from the policy confusion. Middle powers gain from the vacuum.

items.title: New home sales -10.8% YoY; 9.4 months of supply — stagflationary housing freeze
items.idea: New single-family home sales fell -10.8% YoY with 9.4 months of supply — well above the 6-month balanced market threshold. Combined with PCE 3.8%, real income -1.1%, and 30Y above 5%, housing is in a stagflationary freeze. Buyers cannot afford rates. Sellers will not cut. Supply builds. Something breaks — either prices or rates.
items.angle: Housing is a lagging indicator of real economy pain. At 9.4 months supply with record-high prices and 5%+ rates, the freeze eventually cracks through one channel or another.
items.tweetA: New home sales -10.8% YoY. 9.4 months of supply. PCE 3.8%. Real income -1.1%. 30Y above 5%. Housing is in a stagflationary freeze — buyers cannot qualify, sellers will not cut, supply piles up. Warsh inherits this too on June 16.
items.tweetB: 9.4 months of housing supply at prices that need 5%+ mortgages to finance. Either prices break down or rates break down. Those are the only two exits from this freeze. Which one Warsh picks on June 16-17 sets the trajectory for the rest of 2026.
